Object: CI build-result phase-state controller and automatic repair-phase generator
Source objects: CEX12-CEX14, phase table, object register, proof dossiers, implementation scratchpads, workflow artifacts/logs, last green commit and backup ref
Definitions: State machine over CI conclusion, active phase state, backup/work branch/PR identity, failure/success artifacts and planning registers. Red transition sets attempted phase R, emits visible failure-history snapshot evidence, creates/activates a natural-origin repair predecessor and blocks merge/current deploy. Green transition requires build/scanner/artifact/closeout evidence, sets phase G and advances exactly one active cursor.
Proof target: CEX15 may never allow silent status drift: red CI is visible and repair-routed but cannot become production/current; green CI cannot close without evidence or create multiple active cursors.
Proof sketch: Represent the CI conclusion as an input event with commit/workflow/run IDs. Red transition records failed modules/log references, blocks merge/deploy, updates the attempted phase to R, creates a repair phase before the blocked phase with source dependencies/object-link policy/scratchpad/dossier stubs, and activates it if dependencies are known. Green transition records build/scanner/artifact evidence, marks the phase G, updates linked object/dossier/scratchpad rows only through registered evidence fields, computes the first non-green phase whose dependencies are complete, and sets it active while keeping all other non-green phases non-active.
Verification / audit: Planned. Requires red and green fixture runs, manifest validation, YAML patch diffs, failure-history snapshot checks, no-current-deploy checks and cursor uniqueness check.
Next documentation action: Define phase_state_transition_manifest.json and red_failure_snapshot.json schemas, plus dry-run examples for red and green transitions.
Documentation depth requirement: Extended dossier: state-machine definition, transition invariants, failure evidence, repair-phase generation, closeout evidence, cursor uniqueness, and rollback/no-deploy constraints.
